INSTRUCTIONS:
Symmetry Span Task

In this task, you will try to memorise the position of coloured squares you see on the screen while you also make judgements about other pictures.

In the next few minutes you will have a practice task that will allow you to see how this task works.

First, we are going to practice the square memory task.

Click on NEXT to begin.
SQUARES:
Memory Practice

For this practice task, squares will appear on the screen one at a time. Try to remember where each square was, in the order they are presented.

After you see 2-5 squares presented, you will see a grid of the 16 possible places the squares could have been.

Your task: Select each square in the order presented using your mouse. A number will appear to show you the order you have selected.

Click on NEXT.
SQUARES2:
Memory Practice

When you have selected all the squares, and they are in the correct order, hit the DONE button.

  • If you make a mistake, hit the CLEAR button to start over.
  • If you forget one of the squares, click the BLANK button to mark its position.

    Remember: It is very important to get the squares in the same order as you see them. If you forget one, use the BLANK button to mark the position.

    Click on NEXT to begin.
SYMMETRY:
Symmetry Practice

Now you will practice the symmetry part of this task.

A picture will appear on your screen and you will have to decide if it is symmetrical.

A picture is symmetrical if you can fold it in half vertically and the picture on the left lines up with the picture on the right.

On the next screen you will see a picture that is symmetrical.

Click NEXT to continue.
SYMMETRY2:
img_practice/eg1.jpg

Notice that this picture is symmetrical about the red line.

In the real pictures the red line will not be there.

Click NEXT to continue.
SYMMETRY3:
img_practice/eg2.jpg

Here, the picture is NOT left-right symmetrical (even if it is symmetrical top-bottom). If you folded this across the red line, the boxes would NOT line up.

Click NEXT to continue.
SYMMETRY4:
img_practice/eg3.jpg

This is another example of a picture that IS symmetrical. If you folded it vertically, the two sides would line up.

Click NEXT to continue.
SYMMETRY5:
img_practice/eg4.jpg

Here is another example of a picture that is NOT symmetrical. Notice that if you folded it vertically, the two sides would NOT line up.

Click NEXT to continue.
SYMMETRY6:
Symmetry Practice

Once you have decided if the picture is symmetrical, click the mouse.

On the next screen TRUE and FALSE buttons will appear.

  • If the picture was symmetrical, click TRUE
  • If it was NOT symmetrical, click FALSE

    The computer will tell you if you made the right choice.

    It is VERY important that you get the pictures correct.


    Eventually this task will be interleaved with the memory task, so during practice you will see a series of pictures (for now, just 1 or 2) before you get feedback on them all. Later, it will match the length of the list of squares you will be remembering. Click NEXT when you are ready to practice some of the picture problems.
PRACBATTERY1:
Memory + Symmetry Practice

Now you will practice doing both parts of the task at the same time.

In the next practice set, you will be given a symmetry problem. Once you make your decision about the problem, a square will appear on the screen. Try and remember the position of the square.

In the previous section where you only did the symmetry problems, the computer computed your average time to solve the problems. If you take longer than your average time, the computer will automatically move you onto the next square part, thus skipping the True or False part and will count that problem as a symmetry error.

Therefore it is VERY important to solve the symmetry problems as quickly and as accurately as possible.

Click NEXT.
PRACBATTERY2:
Memory + Symmetry Practice

After the square goes away, another symmetry picture will appear, and then another square.

At the end of each set of symmetry pictures and squares, a recall screen will appear. Use the mouse to select the squares you just saw. Try your best to get the squares in the correct order.

Important:
  • Work QUICKLY and ACCURATELY on the symmetry problems
  • Make sure you know the answer before clicking to the next screen
  • You will not be told if your answer is correct

    After the recall screen, you will be given feedback about your performance on both the number of squares recalled and the percent correct on the symmetry problems.

    Click NEXT to continue.
PRACBATTERY3:
Memory + Symmetry Practice

During the feedback, you will see a number in blue in the top right of the screen. This indicates your percent correct for the symmetry problems for the entire experiment.

It is VERY important for you to keep this at least at 85%. For our purposes, we can only use data where the participant was at least 85% accurate on the symmetry pictures.

Therefore, in order for you to be asked to come back for future experiments, you must perform at least at 85% on the symmetry problems WHILE doing your best to recall as many squares as possible.

Raise your hand if you have any questions.

Click NEXT to try some practice problems.
TRIALS:
Begin Main Task

That is the end of the practice.

The real task will look like the practice trials you just completed. First you will get a symmetry problem, then a square to remember.

When you see the recall screen, select the squares in the order presented. If you forget a square, click the BLANK box to mark where it should go.

Some of the sets will have more problems and squares than others.

Remember:
  • Work as QUICKLY and ACCURATELY as possible on symmetry problems
  • Keep your symmetry problem accuracy at 85% or above
  • Do your best to recall as many squares as possible

    Do you have any questions?

    Click NEXT to begin the task.
SECONDCHANCE:
Practice Again

You did not meet the required symmetry accuracy criterion of 85% on that practice round.

It is very important that you maintain at least 85% accuracy on the symmetry problems while also remembering as many squares as possible.

You will now have one more chance to practice the combined task. Please focus on being both quick and accurate on the symmetry judgements.

Click NEXT to try the practice again.
PROCEED_DESPITE_THRESHOLD:
Proceeding to Main Task

You did not meet the required symmetry accuracy criterion of 85% during practice.

However, you will now proceed to the main task. Please remember:

For your data to be used, you must reach at least 85% accuracy on the symmetry problems during the test.

Focus on being both quick and accurate on the symmetry problems while doing your best to recall as many squares as possible.

Click NEXT to begin the main task.
END:
Task Complete!

Well done! You are finished!
FEEDBACK_DT: You got %CORRECT_D%/%NUM_D% problems correct and %CORRECT_T%/%NUM_T% target squares correct
CLICK_PROMPT: Click mouse when you make a decision
